rationale: z3's `unknown` is the standard two-`forall` trigger gap: each duplicated forall ensures triggers on post1_self_.* or post2_self_.*, so the two runs' instantiations never align.  The prelude re-asserts every duplicated forall ensures verbatim for each run (sound — each one is exactly an ensures hypothesis), forcing the triggers to fire, then `=~=`-asserts the directly derivable cross-run equalities (page_mapping, all four domains) so z3 can chain through pre_self_ to align the accessor outputs for the det-equal check.
